chain.thought: Explanation: The race on August 5 is the Marlboro 500, while the race on September 16 is the Red Roof Inns 200. A 500-mile event has more laps than a 200-mile event, so the August 5 race is longer in terms of laps.

judge_thought: Explanation: The August 5 race is the "Marlboro 500" (row 10) and the September 16 race is the "Red Roof Inns 200" (row 13). The numeric values in the race names indicate relative race distance (500 vs 200). Laps are proportional to race distance, so the 500 race would have more laps than the 200 race. Therefore, the August 5 race is longer in terms of laps.
